source_paper_title: Large Independent Sets in Recursive Markov Random Graphs
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Gupte_2025, title={Large Independent Sets in Recursive Markov Random Graphs}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0215}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0215}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Gupte, Akshay and Zhu, Yiran}, year={2025}, month=aug, pages={1611–1634} }
context:
Section 2.2 Discussion, page 7

Question 1. Does there exist a function f(n) = \Omega(n/ \log n) such that f(n) = o(n) and \alpha(G^r_{n,p}) = \Theta(f(n)) w.h.p.?
title: Determine the asymptotic order of maximum independent sets in recursive Markov random graphs
background_latex:
Fix parameters \(p\in(0,1]\) and \(r\in(0,1)\), and write \(\gamma:=1-r\in(0,1)\). Define the random graph \(G^r_{n,p}\) on vertex set \(\{v_1,\dots,v_n\}\) by the following recursive Markov edge-generation rule.

1) For each \(i\in\{2,\dots,n\}\), the edge \((v_1,v_i)\) is present with probability \(p\).

2) For each fixed \(i\in\{3,\dots,n\}\), the edges \((v_j,v_i)\) for \(j=2,3,\dots,i-1\) are generated sequentially as a (non-homogeneous) Markov chain along increasing \(j\): conditional on the previous edge \((v_{j-1},v_i)\),
\[
\mathbb P\big((v_j,v_i)\in E\mid (v_{j-1},v_i)\notin E\big)=\mathbb P\big((v_{j-1},v_i)\in E\big),
\]
\[
\mathbb P\big((v_j,v_i)\in E\mid (v_{j-1},v_i)\in E\big)=r\,\mathbb P\big((v_{j-1},v_i)\in E\big).
\]
Equivalently, if \(X^i_j\in\{0,1\}\) indicates whether \((v_j,v_i)\) is an edge, and \(p^i_j:=\mathbb P(X^i_j=1)\), then \(p^i_1=p\) and for \(2\le j\le i-1\),
\[
 p^i_j = p^i_{j-1}\big(1-\gamma p^i_{j-1}\big).
\]

Let \(\alpha(G)\) denote the stability number (maximum cardinality of an independent set) of a graph \(G\). The asymptotic regime is \(n\to\infty\); “with high probability (w.h.p.)” means with probability tending to 1 as \(n\to\infty\).
problem_definition_latex:
Find an explicit function \(f(n)\) such that
\[
 f(n)=\Omega\!\left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right),\qquad f(n)=o(n),
\]
and
\[
\alpha\big(G^r_{n,p}\big)=\Theta\big(f(n)\big)\quad\text{w.h.p. as }n\to\infty.
\]
(Here \(p\in(0,1]\) and \(r\in(0,1)\) are fixed constants and \(G^r_{n,p}\) is as defined above.)
latex:
Fix parameters \(p\in(0,1]\) and \(r\in(0,1)\), and write \(\gamma:=1-r\in(0,1)\). Define the random graph \(G^r_{n,p}\) on vertex set \(\{v_1,\dots,v_n\}\) by the following recursive Markov edge-generation rule.

1) For each \(i\in\{2,\dots,n\}\), the edge \((v_1,v_i)\) is present with probability \(p\).

2) For each fixed \(i\in\{3,\dots,n\}\), the edges \((v_j,v_i)\) for \(j=2,3,\dots,i-1\) are generated sequentially as a (non-homogeneous) Markov chain along increasing \(j\): conditional on the previous edge \((v_{j-1},v_i)\),
\[
\mathbb P\big((v_j,v_i)\in E\mid (v_{j-1},v_i)\notin E\big)=\mathbb P\big((v_{j-1},v_i)\in E\big),
\]
\[
\mathbb P\big((v_j,v_i)\in E\mid (v_{j-1},v_i)\in E\big)=r\,\mathbb P\big((v_{j-1},v_i)\in E\big).
\]
Equivalently, if \(X^i_j\in\{0,1\}\) indicates whether \((v_j,v_i)\) is an edge, and \(p^i_j:=\mathbb P(X^i_j=1)\), then \(p^i_1=p\) and for \(2\le j\le i-1\),
\[
 p^i_j = p^i_{j-1}\big(1-\gamma p^i_{j-1}\big).
\]

Let \(\alpha(G)\) denote the stability number (maximum cardinality of an independent set) of a graph \(G\). The asymptotic regime is \(n\to\infty\); “with high probability (w.h.p.)” means with probability tending to 1 as \(n\to\infty\).

Find an explicit function \(f(n)\) such that
\[
 f(n)=\Omega\!\left(\frac{n}{\log n}\right),\qquad f(n)=o(n),
\]
and
\[
\alpha\big(G^r_{n,p}\big)=\Theta\big(f(n)\big)\quad\text{w.h.p. as }n\to\infty.
\]
(Here \(p\in(0,1]\) and \(r\in(0,1)\) are fixed constants and \(G^r_{n,p}\) is as defined above.)
open_problem_quote: Question 1. Does there exist a function f(n) = \Omega(n/ \log n) such that f(n) = o(n) and \alpha(G^r_{n,p}) = \Theta(f(n)) w.h.p.?
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can simulate the random graph process for moderate n and estimate \(\alpha(G^r_{n,p})\) via heuristics/ILP to guess scaling, but verifying a \(\Theta(f(n))\) law w.h.p. is asymptotic and not falsified by a single finite instance. Still, computational experiments can search for plausible growth rates and constants, or suggest tighter upper/lower bounds.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: A coding-agent loop can generate candidate scaling hypotheses \(f(n)\) (e.g., \(n/\log n\), \(n/(\log n)^a\), \(n^{\beta}\), etc.) and fit them against computed/estimated \(\alpha(G^r_{n,p})\) over many simulated graphs, using regression error as a fitness signal. This can guide conjectures and suggest proof targets, though it cannot directly certify w.h.p. \(\Theta(\cdot)\) behavior.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
